final_arbitration.reasoning: Both teachers read the same SURG20 recording with similar phase structures. 5s delta within expected noise. Midpoint reconciliation.
Recomputed: 600 - 138.5 - 3.5 = 458.0. Delta of 4.0 FLS among smallest in series.

knot_assessments.notes: Surgeon's knot - both teachers agree on double wrap.
Single throw with hand switch - both confirm alternating pattern.
Single throw switched back. Secure knot stack.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Both teachers agree - no visible gap, complete closure, no avulsion.

consensus_score.technique_summary: V_8 (SURG20 wall-mount) is a strong top-tier performance at ~138s. Both teachers converge on: fast needle load, efficient placement, consistent throw pattern, and a prolonged 45s cut phase that dominates end-of-task time. Drain outcome is clean. Cross-station skill transfer demonstration.
improvement_suggestions: 45s cut phase is 33% of total time - primary bottleneck
Third throw at 28s is 8s slower than throws 1-2
Oblique wall-mount angle - direct camera would improve scoring confidence
strengths: Clean drain closure with no visible gap

critique_reasoning: Round 1 early-stopped due to very_strong teacher agreement. No arbitration needed beyond midpoint reconciliation.
